name: Terrance Brennan's Ricotta and Swiss Chard Gnocchi
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound Yukon Gold or Idaho potatoes, peeled, cooked
3 cups Lightly packed fresh Swiss chard, cooked and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Pass the hot potatoes through a food mill and cool. Lightly knead in the flour.
2. Add the egg yolk, ricotta, and grated aged ricotta cheese to the dough. Knead lightly.
3. Add the chopped Swiss chard, Parmigiano Reggiano, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Mix well and knead until smooth. Do not over-knead.
4. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface into 1/2-inch ropes. Cut into 3/4-inch lengths. Chill for at least 45 minutes.
5. Bring 3 quarts of salted water to a boil. Add the gnocchi in three batches and cook until they rise to the top. Remove with a slotted spoon.
6. Warm the chicken broth and whisk in the garlic puree, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Divide the gnocchi into 8 dishes. Ladle with the broth and garnish with Prosciutto and cheese.
Note: To roast garlic, remove the papery skin from the whole head. Slice off 1/4-inch across the top. Place in a small baking dish and drizzle with olive oil. Cover with foil and cook for 30 minutes at 350°F. Remove foil and cook 20 minutes longer, or until soft.
